source.name: TDD-Bench-Verified
source.relationship: All cases are members of the upstream verified ID list; fix and gold-test records are intentionally omitted from this public manifest.

criteria: Public GitHub issue in a Python repository with pytest-compatible tests.
Exact buggy base commit and a human fix/test pair exist in TDD-Bench-Verified.
The issue body does not contain a complete executable test function with its final assertion.
The historical environment passed upstream verification and is suitable for containerized replay.
The set spans exceptions, wrong values, serialization, collection, configuration, mutation, formatting, and validation behavior.
exclusions: Django cases whose canonical issue is a Trac ticket rather than a GitHub issue.
Cases requiring a live external service, GPU, or network during verification.
Cases with a complete copy-paste reproduction test in the issue body.
Cases estimated by upstream reviewers to require more than one hour to fix.
Any case inspected using ReproAssert output before this manifest was frozen.
limitations: This is a deliberately small, easy-to-medium feasibility cohort, not a representative sample of all GitHub issues.
The source issues and their historical fixes are public and may have appeared in model training data.
Cases must not be replaced after a failed run; corrections require a new benchmark version or a documented erratum.

evaluator_only_fields: fixing pull request URL and number
fixed commit and production fix patch

claim_ladder.name: executable_candidate
claim_ladder.definition: The test-only patch applies, compiles or collects, and fails repeatably on the exact buggy base for an issue-aligned observed symptom.
claim_ladder.public_ceiling: This is the highest claim available before a fix exists.

claim_ladder.name: plausible_f2p
claim_ladder.definition: L0 plus repeated passing executions after the evaluator applies the hidden human production fix.
claim_ladder.public_ceiling: Fail-to-pass is necessary evidence but is not semantic correctness.

claim_ladder.name: semantic_valid
claim_ladder.definition: L1 plus blinded review and causal controls showing that the trigger and oracle encode the issue rather than incidental patch drift.
claim_ladder.public_ceiling: This is the benchmark's primary success definition.

claim_ladder.name: maintainer_validated
claim_ladder.definition: A real maintainer independently validates or accepts the generated reproduction.
claim_ladder.public_ceiling: This claim requires separately authorized outreach and recorded external evidence.

outcome_taxonomy.definition: The benchmark environment or evaluator failed independently of the candidate; repair and rerun before closing the scored run.
No candidate patch was produced within budget.
The candidate is empty, malformed, or cannot be applied to the base tree.
The candidate edits forbidden production/configuration/dependency files or contains prohibited test behavior.
The candidate cannot reach test collection because of its own setup, dependency, or top-level execution error.
The intended generated test node cannot compile or collect.
The candidate runs successfully but does not fail on the buggy base.
The candidate fails repeatably on the base for a syntax, import, setup, timeout, generic crash, or other symptom unrelated to the issue.
The candidate does not produce a consistent issue-aligned failure across all required base repetitions.
The candidate continues to fail after the hidden production fix is applied.
The candidate does not pass consistently across all required fixed repetitions.
The candidate is mechanically fail-to-pass but review or causal controls show that it does not faithfully reproduce the issue.
The candidate meets the L2 definition and counts toward the primary benchmark metric.

gates.interpretation: Passing the internal 6/20 gate supports continuing the product bet; it does not establish a stable 30 percent population success rate.

attributable_cost_includes: model inference for all successful and failed attempts
marginal sandbox compute for generation and verification
paid storage or artifact transfer incurred by the scored run
reported_separately: one-time dependency image construction cost and time
human benchmark curation and semantic review labor
required_statistics: p50 and p90 cost per case
p50 and p90 generation and verification wall time
cost_semantics.warning: Median cost among successful cases alone is not sufficient because it hides spend on failures.

contamination.caveat: All issues and fixes are historical public GitHub data. Model pretraining exposure cannot be ruled out, so this benchmark must not be described as contamination-free or state of the art.
controls: Pin the exact buggy base SHA and issue snapshot.
Disable generator network access after bounded dependency preparation.
Do not mount evaluator fix, gold-test, source-record, or review artifacts into the generator sandbox.
Record model/provider/version, prompt hash, configuration hash, and run date.
Publish every frozen case outcome and never replace a failure post hoc.
residual_risks: A model may have memorized issue text, repository code, or the human solution during pretraining.
The manually selected easy-to-medium cohort may overestimate performance on ambiguous or operationally complex issues.
Public base SHAs and issue URLs make the hidden fix discoverable outside the isolated generation process.

cases.title: IndexError: tuple index out of range in identify_format (io.registry)
In v5.3, NDDataRef mask propagation fails when one operand does not have a mask
Unit equality comparison with None raises TypeError for UnrecognizedUnit
ax.bar raises for all-NaN data on matplotlib 3.6.1
Unable to pickle a figure with a draggable legend
GaussianMixture predict and fit_predict disagree when n_init is greater than 1
TypeError when supplying a boolean X to HuberRegressor.fit
IndexError in export_text when a tree has only one feature
ValueError while collecting tests that patch an array
Symlinked directories are not collected since pytest 6.1.0
Boolean weights produce incorrect weighted means
to_unstacked_dataset is broken for single-dimension variables
swap_dims can modify the original object
Removing a default header from a session
A request with a binary payload fails during native-string conversion
mathematica_code produces incorrect output for Max
is_zero is incorrect on a complex integer expression
Blueprints should require a non-empty name
Numeric legends show wrong values for large ranges
Setting min-similarity-lines to 0 should disable duplicate-code checking
